channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: 40 Uyghur men detained in Thailand for more than a decade have been deported to China
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — A group of 40 Uyghur men detained in Thailand for more than a decade have been deported to China, Thai and Chinese officials said Thursday. The men made a public appeal last month to halt the deportation, saying they faced imprisonment and possible death in China.
item.dc:creator: DAKE KANG and HUIZHONG WU, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Driver in Israel injures 9 in suspected ramming attack: police
item.description: A driver in Israel plowed into a bus station, injuring nine people, including one person critically, in what police suspect was a ramming attack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration eliminating 90 percent of USAID foreign aid contracts
item.description: President Trump’s administration is eliminating more than 90 percent of the U.S. Agency for International Development's (USAID) foreign contracts, along with $60 billion in global aid worldwide, multiple outlets reported.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Pope Francis' slight kidney insufficiency has receded as he battles pneumonia, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis slept well and was resting Thursday morning as his doctors say the 88-year-old pontiff has shown further slight improvement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel's refusal to withdraw from this narrow strip of desert could threaten the Gaza ceasefire
item.description: Israel's refusal to withdraw from a narrow strip of desert on the Gaza side of the border with Egypt, as called for in the ceasefire with Hamas, could further threaten the fragile truce.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Key Pakistan-Afghanistan border crossing remains shut, disrupting trade and movement of people
item.description: Officials say a nearly week-long closure of a key crossing on the Afghanistan-Pakistan border has disrupted bilateral trade and the movement of people.
item.dc:creator: Riaz Khan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Russian and US diplomats meet in Istanbul for second round of talks
item.description: Russian and U.S. diplomats have held talks in Istanbul to discuss ways to normalize the operation of their respective embassies after rounds of diplomatic expulsions in recent years.
item.dc:creator: Mehmet Guzel, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The EU beat COVID and ditched Russian energy. Now it must learn to live without US security help
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — Over the last five years, European Union countries have been forced to adapt to unprecedented circumstances. They pulled together to purchase tens of millions of vaccines and devised an innovative debt financing scheme to resuscitate their COVID-19-ravaged economies.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: EU pushes back hard against Trump tariff threats and his caustic comments that bloc is out to get US
item.description: The European Union is insisting that the 27-nation bloc is not out to undermine the United States as U.S. President Donald Trump put it.
item.dc:creator: Raf Casert, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 84 Indonesians taken out of scam centers in Myanmar are set to go home
item.description: MAE SOT, Thailand (AP) — Eighty-four Indonesians freed from scam centers in Myanmar arrived at the Thai border city of Mae Sot earlier on Thursday in an ongoing repatriation effort that follows a crackdown by Thailand, Myanmar and China.
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fire kills 8 in the Philippines after flames gutted a residential building in less than an hour
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — A fire gutted a three-story residential building early Thursday in less than an hour in the Philippines' capital region, killing eight people, officials said.
item.dc:creator: JOEAL CALUPITAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Andrew Tate, who faces rape, trafficking charges in Romania, has left for US
item.description: The brothers are also charged with forming a criminal gang to sexually exploit women. Andrew Tate also faces an additional charge of rape.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH and ANDREEA ALEXANDRU, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Oscar-winner Gene Hackman, wife Betsy Arakawa and their dog were dead for some time, warrant shows
item.description: A search warrant shows that Oscar-winner Gene Hackman, his wife and their dog had been dead for some time and the couple’s bodies were in different rooms.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pope beats back speculation of imminent death or conclave as he continues pneumonia recovery
item.description: Pope Francis is continuing his slow recovery from double pneumonia and beating back speculation of an imminent death, resignation or conclave.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Winfield, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: UK's Starmer meets with Trump as Europe's leaders worry about drifting US support for Ukraine
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer is visiting the White House to try to convince President Donald Trump that peace in Ukraine will endure only if Kyiv and European leaders are at the negotiating table along with Moscow.
item.dc:creator: Jill Lawless And Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hamas hands over bodies of 4 hostages to Israel as dozens of Palestinians leave Israeli prison
item.description: KHAN YOUNIS, Gaza Strip (AP) — Hamas handed over the bodies of four hostages to the Red Cross early Thursday in exchange for Israel's release of hundreds of Palestinian prisoners, days before the first phase of the ceasefire in the Gaza Strip was to end.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, SAMY MAGDY and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says Zelenskyy is coming to the White House to sign US-Ukraine critical minerals deal
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said Wednesday that Ukrainian leader Volodymyr Zelenskyy will visit the White House on Friday to sign a long-sought minerals deal that will closely tie the two countries together for years to come.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, HANNA ARHIROVA and JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Romanian prosecutors launch investigation into far-right populist who led canceled presidential vote
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — Romanian prosecutors launched a criminal investigation on Wednesday against a far-right populist who won the first round in a canceled presidential election last year, accusing him of election campaign funding abuses, supporting fascist groups and other offenses.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH and ANDREEA ALEXANDRU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rubio: Ukraine rare earth minerals deal a 'tush push' away
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio on Wednesday said President Trump is "very close" to making a deal to gain access to Ukraine's critical minerals as he pushes to end the war with Russia, referencing the Philadelphia Eagle's signature "tush push" strategy.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Romanian far-right presidential frontrunner taken into custody after JD Vance's rebuke of European elections
item.description: Romanian candidate Calin Georgescu is being questioned by prosecutors after Vice President JD Vance criticized a "Soviet-era" shift in European democracies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mass crackdowns see thousands of scam center workers waiting in Myanmar for repatriation
item.description: MAE SOT, Thailand (AP) — A new crackdown on online scam centers has led to over 7,000 people from around the world being held in a Myanmar border town awaiting repatriation, and those helping them say the unprecedented number is straining the resources of Thailand just across the border and leading
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI and HUIZHONG WU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: At least four dead in South Korea highway construction project collapse
item.description: At least four people were killed and six others were injured after concrete structures supporting a bridge collapsed at a highway construction site in South Korea.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former Hamas hostage briefs UN Security Council on the 'pure hell' that was captivity in Gaza
item.description: Former Hamas hostage Noa Argamani spoke to the U.N. Security Council on Tuesday about her time as a captive in Gaza. She also made a desperate plea for the release of the remaining hostages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says minerals deal has been 'pretty much' negotiated with Zelenskyy, meeting slated for Friday
item.description: President Donald Trump and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy have agreed to the terms of a minerals deal, allowing U.S. access to Ukrainian natural resources.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Man forced to sit next to corpse after woman died midway through flight
item.description: Passenger Mitchell Ring said the flight crew did everything they could to revive the woman but they were unsuccessful.
item.dc:creator: Emily Smith, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis in critical but stable condition, has CT scan, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis remains in critical condition but is stable and returned to work Tuesday evening, the Vatican announced.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy says he'd be ready to give up presidency if it brought peace, NATO membership
item.description: “If to achieve peace, you really need me to give up my post, I’m ready,” Zelenskyy said in response to a journalist's question on whether he’d trade his office for peace. “I can trade it for NATO.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Afghan women’s radio station will resume broadcasts after Taliban lift suspension
item.description: Radio Begum launched on International Women’s Day in March 2021, five months before the Taliban seized power.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Vatican says Pope Francis is in critical condition
item.description: The 88-year-old Francis, who has been hospitalized for a week with a complex lung infection, also received blood transfusions after tests showed a condition associated with anemia, the Vatican said in a late update.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Winfield, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Delta faces first lawsuits days after plane flips in Toronto
item.description: All 21 people injured in the crash landing have been released from the hospital, and no one was killed.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Israel identifies remains of child hostages but says another body from Hamas was not their mother
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — The Israeli military said Friday it had positively identified the remains of two young hostages but another body released by Hamas under a ceasefire deal was not the boys’ mother as the militant group had promised.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, JOSEF FEDERMAN AND WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, WGN 9
